signals.headline: Ripple Releases Four-Phase Quantum-Resistant Roadmap for XRPL | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Ripple Releases Four-Phase Quantum-Resistant Roadmap for XRPL | YES | HIGH confidence**

Ripple announced a four-phase plan to make XRPL quantum-resistant by 2028, positioning it ahead of Bitcoin and Ethereum on quantum security timelines[1][3]. Phase 2 is already underway with completion targeted for H1 2026, Phase 3 deployment to testnet in H2 2026, and full network implementation by 2028[1].

signals.headline: Remittix Demonstrates PayFi Platform with Real-Time Crypto-to-Fiat Transfers | YES | MEDIUM confidence**
signals.raw:
Remittix Demonstrates PayFi Platform with Real-Time Crypto-to-Fiat Transfers | YES | MEDIUM confidence**

Remittix showcased concrete footage of its PayFi platform demonstrating real-time crypto-to-fiat conversion capabilities (announced April 20)[2], moving the project beyond concept into visible product progress.

signals.headline: XRP Flips BNB to Third Largest Cryptocurrency | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
XRP Flips BNB to Third Largest Cryptocurrency | YES | HIGH confidence**

XRP climbed to an $87.5B market cap, overtaking BNB for third place behind Bitcoin and Ethereum[1]. This move was accompanied by three major catalysts: XRPL transaction volume topped $1B, and ETF inflows reached $1.2B in a single week[1]. The price movement to $1.4293 (up 0.30%) has on-chain usage supporting it rather than pu

signals.headline: XRP's MACD Flipped Bullish | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
XRP's MACD Flipped Bullish | YES | HIGH confidence**

XRP's daily MACD indicator flipped bullish in mid-April for the first time since January, when the same signal triggered a 25% rally to $2.40[5]. This technical milestone occurred after three months of sell signals.

signals.headline: Russia GDP Contracts 1
signals.raw:
Russia GDP Contracts 1.8% Jan-Feb 2026, Putin Confirms Decline** | YES | MED[2]

Putin acknowledged in a recent economic meeting that Russia's GDP fell 1.8% year-over-year in January-February 2026 versus 2025, with manufacturing and construction losses, after 1% growth in 2025; Central Bank forecasts Q1 growth at 1.6% but Academy of Sciences predicts -1.5%[2]. Federal budget revenues dropped 8.2%

signals.headline: US Q4 2025 GDP Growth Downgraded to 0
signals.raw:
US Q4 2025 GDP Growth Downgraded to 0.5% in Third Estimate** | YES | HIGH[1]

The US Bureau of Economic Analysis released its third estimate on April 9, 2026, confirming Q4 2025 GDP growth at a slower 0.5% annualized rate, down from prior figures, amid consumer resilience but broader slowdown signals[1].
